summary.intent: Address Codex review findings on PR #679 in fogodev/ars-ui, specifically fixing roving tabindex and state reset bugs in MenuBar and ContextOpen components, then get CI green and the PR approved.
summary.outcome: Fixed disable→re-enable tab stop restoration in MenuBar's sync_props_plan, committed as 'fix(selection): restore menu bar tab stop after reenable' (pushed to remote), replied to and resolved all open Codex review threads, and re-triggered Codex for another pass. CI was progressing through unit tests and mutual exclusion guard checks at session end.

repo: MenuBar roving tabindex must be explicitly restored on re-enable — SyncProps clears focus on disable but did not previously restore a tab stop when the component is re-enabled.
ContextOpen requires transient submenu and typeahead state to be reset when the menu is reopened, not just when it closes.
The project uses a multi-stage CI pipeline: static checks (clippy/workspace) run before unit tests and a 'Mutual Exclusion Guard' job.
Codex review is triggered via '@codex review' PR comment and signals activity via GitHub reactions (eyes emoji) before posting new review threads.
The repo uses cargo xtask spec validate for spec file validation alongside fmt and clippy checks before committing.
The project has proptest state machine tests for selection components, run with --ignored flag targeting specific component names.

code.finding: sync_props_plan area — disable path clears focused trigger but re-enable path was missing logic to restore a roving tab stop; fixed in this session.
Spec file was updated alongside the implementation fix to document the re-enable tab stop restoration behavior.

workflow: The team uses a poll-then-act loop: check reactions for Codex activity signal, then fetch unresolved threads via GraphQL, then read CI status — all in one compound shell command with sleep intervals.
Codex review threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation immediately after posting a reply comment.
Commits co-authored with 'Codex <codex@openai.com>' to attribute AI-assisted fixes.
cargo +nightly fmt --all is used for formatting, with --check used pre-commit to verify; cargo xtask and git diff --check are also part of the pre-commit gate.
Test command pattern: cargo test -p <crate> <module_path> --features i18n, with proptest invocation using -- --ignored <component_name>.

friction: Codex review passes are slow — multiple 2-minute polling cycles were needed between triggering '@codex review' and seeing new threads appear.
Compound shell poll commands are fragile and verbose; any one sub-command failure silently drops that section of output.
Re-enable fix required reading specific line ranges of mod.rs manually (nl -ba + sed) rather than having direct IDE navigation.
open_items: CI was still running (Unit Tests and Mutual Exclusion Guard in progress) at session end — final green status not yet confirmed.
Codex was still in an active review pass at session end — additional findings may still be posted that require follow-up.
PR #679 merge readiness (mergeStateStatus) was not yet confirmed as MERGEABLE at session end.
